title.purpose: Selected belief name in the detail panel — largest text in the app.

body.purpose: Notes, references, menu items, button labels. Cap prose at 65–75ch.

label.purpose: Form field labels and section headers, often in Ink Muted.

node-label.purpose: Belief name under each donut; iris + bold when selected.

shadows.name: floating
shadows.purpose: Soft near-black shadow that grounds floating surfaces (modal, detail panel, dropdown, Flow controls/minimap) against the dark field. Depth is carried by surface lightness, not this.

shadows.name: node-lift
shadows.purpose: Soft dark shadow seating each glowing white donut disc on the field.

shadows.name: moon-glow
shadows.purpose: Fixed violet wash behind the field — lighter at top, darker at edges — giving the night sky depth. Single hue family only.

shadows.name: backdrop-scrim
shadows.purpose: Dims the field behind an open modal.

elevation.name: surface
elevation.purpose: Panels, inputs, app bar — lifts by being lighter than base.

elevation.name: overlay
elevation.purpose: Dropdowns, node ± badge, toasts — the top of the tonal ladder.

motion.name: node-inflate
motion.purpose: Belief node radius/stroke growth on hover (25px → 37px).

motion.name: press-nudge
motion.purpose: 1px downward translate on button :active for tactile press.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single main action in a context (Add premise, Save changes). Iris fill with dark text.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.4rem; background: #C4A7E7; border: 1px solid #C4A7E7; color: #232136; padding: 0.5rem 0.9rem; border-radius: 8px; font: 600 0.9rem system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: filter 0.15s ease, transform 0.05s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { filter: brightness(1.08); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid rgba(196,167,231,0.6); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Default Button
components.description: Quiet secondary action on the dark theme — dark surface, border shifts to iris on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.4rem; background: #2A273F; border: 1px solid #44415A; color: #E0DEF4; padding: 0.5rem 0.9rem; border-radius: 8px; font: 500 0.9rem system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 0.15s ease, transform 0.05s ease; } .ds-btn:hover { border-color: #C4A7E7; } .ds-btn:active { transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid rgba(196,167,231,0.6); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Danger Button
components.description: Destructive action only (delete). Love-pink text + tinted border, love wash on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-danger { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.4rem; background: #2A273F; border: 1px solid color-mix(in srgb, #EB6F92 40%, #44415A); color: #EB6F92; padding: 0.5rem 0.9rem; border-radius: 8px; font: 500 0.9rem system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s ease, border-color 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-danger:hover { border-color: #EB6F92; background: color-mix(in srgb, #EB6F92 8%, #2A273F); }

components.name: Text Field
components.description: Dark labeled input with a soft iris focus glow; label above in muted ink.
components.css: .ds-field { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.3rem; } .ds-field span { font: 600 0.8rem system-ui, sans-serif; color: #908CAA; } .ds-input { font: 400 0.95rem system-ui, sans-serif; padding: 0.55rem 0.7rem; border: 1px solid #44415A; border-radius: 8px; background: #2A273F; color: #E0DEF4; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #908CAA; } .ds-input:focus { outline: 2px solid rgba(196,167,231,0.4); outline-offset: 0; border-color: #C4A7E7; }

components.name: Source Chip
components.description: Pill tag for a belief's source: colored icon + tinted pill + hairline border carry the hue; the label stays in ink.
components.html: Logical reasoning
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.4rem; font: 600 0.8rem system-ui, sans-serif; color: #E0DEF4; background: color-mix(in srgb, #C4A7E7 14%, transparent); border: 1px solid color-mix(in srgb, #C4A7E7 32%, transparent); padding: 0.3rem 0.6rem; border-radius: 999px; }

components.name: Confidence Gauge
components.description: Three pips beside the source chip; filled pips take the source hue, empty pips are a wash of muted ink. Restates the confidence axis that the donut opacity encodes.
components.css: .ds-gauge { display: inline-flex; gap: 3px; } .ds-pip { width: 16px; height: 6px; border-radius: 3px; background: color-mix(in srgb, #908CAA 22%, transparent); } .ds-pip.on { background: #C4A7E7; }

components.name: Floating Panel
components.description: The belief detail panel — a surface card that lifts above the darker field by tone, with a soft grounding shadow.
components.html:
Direct observation

Coffee helps me focus

A pattern I notice most mornings.
components.css: .ds-panel { background: #2A273F; border: 1px solid #44415A; border-radius: 12px; box-shadow: 0 14px 32px rgba(11,10,20,0.55); padding: 1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem; max-width: 320px; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-panel-chip { display: inline-block; font: 600 0.8rem system-ui, sans-serif; color: #E0DEF4; background: color-mix(in srgb, #3E8FB0 14%, transparent); border: 1px solid color-mix(in srgb, #3E8FB0 32%, transparent); padding: 0.3rem 0.6rem; border-radius: 999px; } .ds-panel h2 { font: 600 1.15rem system-ui, sans-serif; color: #E0DEF4; margin: 0.75rem 0 0.5rem; } .ds-panel p { font: 400 0.95rem/1.5 system-ui, sans-serif; color: #E0DEF4; margin: 0; }

components.name: Belief Node (Donut)
components.description: The signature object: a white-filled SVG donut that glows on the dark field; arc hue = source, arc width = count, arc opacity = confidence.
components.html: <svg class="ds-node" width="120" height="120" viewBox="-60 -60 120 120" role="img" aria-label="Belief: example"><defs><filter id="ds-node-shadow" x="-50%" y="-50%" width="200%" height="200%"><feDropShadow dx="0" dy="4" stdDeviation="10" flood-color="#000000" flood-opacity="0.45"></feDropShadow></filter></defs><circle r="25" fill="white" stroke="#9CCFD8" stroke-width="1" stroke-opacity="0.35" filter="url(#ds-node-shadow)"></circle><path d="M 0 -25 A 25 25 0 0 0 -25 0" fill="none" stroke="#9CCFD8" stroke-width="13"></path><path d="M -25 0 A 25 25 0 0 0 0 25" fill="none" stroke="#3E8FB0" stroke-width="10"></path><path d="M 0 25 A 25 25 0 0 0 25 0" fill="none" stroke="#C4A7E7" stroke-width="10" stroke-opacity="0.68"></path><path d="M 25 0 A 25 25 0 0 0 0 -25" fill="none" stroke="#F6C177" stroke-width="10"></path><circle r="8.3" fill="white" stroke="#C4A7E7" stroke-width="2.5"></circle><text y="41" text-anchor="middle" font-family="system-ui, sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="500" fill="#E0DEF4">Example belief</text></svg>
components.css: .ds-node circle, .ds-node path { transition: r 0.18s ease, stroke-width 0.18s ease; } .ds-node { cursor: pointer; }

narrative.overview: Belief Map is a single, full-bleed dark field you pan and zoom across, scattered with small glowing charts — each belief a white donut whose arcs show what its supporting premises are made of. The theme is Rose Pine (Moon): a deep muted-violet base IS the night-sky field, and the white donuts glow on top of it like moons, ringed in Rose Pine's soft accent hues. Everything that isn't the constellation is quiet dark chrome that lifts above the field by lightness rather than heavy shadow. Belief data is encoded on two axes: source category (where the warrant comes from) paints the arc hue, and confidence (how strongly held) sets the arc opacity. Both axes are a per-map editable taxonomy — categories and confidence levels travel in the map JSON and can be swapped via a preset (Source of justification, Epistemic ladder, DIKW tiers) or edited one at a time; the default preset uses Rose Pine's accent set. The feel is cozy, unhurried, and low-stakes to tinker with, in the spirit of a canvas-native tool like tldraw — a personal night-sky you sketch into, not an instrument that judges you.
keyCharacteristics: Full-bleed dark pannable field is the primary surface; all chrome floats above it.
Rose Pine Moon palette; a single iris accent in the chrome, its accent set for the data.
White donut discs glow against the dark base — the constellation reads as luminous.
Compact, functional type on a system-UI stack — legibility over display drama.
Dark surfaces lift by lightness (base → surface → overlay), not by heavy shadow.
Two data axes: source category = arc hue, confidence = arc opacity. Always icon + label.
Per-map editable taxonomy (categories + confidence levels), preset-seeded, autosaved.

rules.name: The Equal-Categories Rule
rules.body: All source categories are peers. Never size, order, or style one to imply its warrant is more valid than another. The donut reports the mix; it never grades it.

rules.name: The Two-Axis Rule
rules.body: Hue = source, opacity = confidence. A tentative belief's arc is paler (~0.4), an established one is full (1.0). Never overload hue with confidence or vice-versa, and never let a low-opacity arc fade so far it reads as absent — 0.4 is the floor.

rules.name: The Icon-and-Label Rule
rules.body: A source is always backed by its icon and label, never color alone — chips (colored icon + tinted pill + ink label), forms, and the node's own name below the donut. Meaning-bearing text stays in ink so it clears contrast on any hue.

rules.name: The Lift-by-Light Rule
rules.body: On this dark theme depth comes from surface lightness (base → surface → overlay), not shadow. Shadows only soften the edge of things that truly float; they never carry elevation on their own.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: No second typeface. If something needs to stand out, change weight or size — never introduce a display or mono font. The system UI stack is the voice.

rules.name: The Compact-Ceiling Rule
rules.body: Nothing exceeds ~1.15rem. This is a working canvas, not a landing page; large display type would break the tool-disappears principle.

dos: Do keep the source colors as visual equals, each always paired with its icon and label.
Do hold the two axes separate: hue = source, opacity = confidence (0.4 floor, 1.0 full); restate confidence explicitly (the gauge) where there's room.
Do convey depth by surface lightness first (base → surface → overlay); keep the soft 0 14px 32px rgba(11,10,20,0.55) shadow only on things that truly float.
Do keep the donut fills white so they glow against the dark base.
Do drive hierarchy with weight and size on the single system-UI family — 400 / 600 / 700, nothing over ~1.15rem.
Do put actions on the object the user selected (detail panel), not a persistent toolbar.
Do give every animation a prefers-reduced-motion fallback — node inflate, modal pop, and toast fly must degrade to instant or crossfade.
Do keep motion soft — the 1px press nudge, the 0.18s node inflate — springy, never bouncy or elastic.
donts: Don't build cold enterprise SaaS: no dashboard grids, no hero-metric tiles, no corporate-blue chrome, no identical card grids.
Don't be preachy or persuasive: never rank, score, or visually privilege one source over another.
Don't become a cluttered power-tool: no dense always-on toolbars, no tiny controls crammed edge to edge.
Don't go childish or gimmicky: no cartoon mascots, no novelty animation, no bounce/elastic easing.
Don't rely on color alone for any source, and don't let a low-confidence arc fade below 0.4 opacity into looking absent.
Don't invert the palette into a fake light mode or chase depth with heavier shadows instead of lighter surfaces — this is a committed dark (Rose Pine Moon) theme.
Don't add a second font family, or any display/hero type over ~1.15rem.
Don't use a colored border-left/border-right stripe as an accent, and don't let the field-background rings exceed their faint role (~7% opacity, pointer-events: none).
